title: Picris turcomanica
taxonomy: Picris turcomanica is a species of plant in the dandelion tribe within the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Picris, which includes several species of dandelion-like plants. The genus Picris is part of the tribe Cichorieae, which is a group of flowering plants known for their dandelion-like appearance. Picris turcomanica is native to the region of Central Asia, specifically in Turkmenistan. The classification of Picris turcomanica reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asteraceae family.

intro: Picris turcomanica, a member of the Asteraceae family, is a perennial plant native to Central Asia, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves with a distinctive bitter taste. Traditionally, Picris turcomanica has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Picris turcomanica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Picris turcomanica is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are valued for their potential to reduce inflammation and boost the immune system.
